notes: F010 is Sprint-1-carry-over infra tooling (D062). Closes review-chain §6.3's automation gap flagged in D047. No user-facing surface, so the abbreviated chain applies per protocol: CPO -> CTO -> Backend -> QA -> CPO-signoff. No UX Researcher, no Legal, no Security stage. All eight test cases from the spec must land plus the current repo must exit 0 with the new audit.
acceptance: scripts/check_claim_register.py exists and exits 0 on the current repo
scripts/git-hooks/pre-commit template exists and is executable
scripts/install_git_hooks.py installs idempotently on a temp repo and refuses to clobber a foreign pre-commit hook without backup
tests/platform/test_claim_register_audit.py holds >= 8 tests including a red-line SPARE_CLAIM case
Adding a fake SPARE_CLAIM constant to a platform module fails the audit with a diagnostic
invariants: Inv#2 (D065): does NOT touch agent/live/*, agent/risk/*, agent/squad/*
Never auto-installs the git-hook; users opt in via `python3 scripts/install_git_hooks.py`
Legal owns the register text -- --fix mode NOT implemented
